mnemonics: My Own Classes Exist Right Outside Network
keyPoints: The 'type' keyword is used to specify the type of a variable, function, or class.
The 'export' keyword is used to make a function or variable accessible from outside the current file or module.
The 'export' keyword is not used to inherit classes or use inheritance.
The 'export' keyword is used with classes to make them accessible to other files or modules.
The 'type' keyword is a reserved keyword in C++, it cannot be used as a variable or function name.
